human_verification.notes: Reviewed the package for delivery-and-takeout specificity, authority-source quality, dispatch and handoff-state clarity, refund and fee boundary handling, fraud and food-safety escalation posture, and validated spec-pack completeness.

specialist_owned_evidence: Boundary is explicit to restaurant delivery and takeout execution, dispatch and handoff tracking, completion evidence, and exception routing rather than generic guest support or broad logistics dispatch.

Authority sources cover FDA retail-food workflow and employee-health guidance, ServSafe delivery workflow guidance, FTC restaurant-delivery consumer-protection enforcement, CFPB billing-error guidance, California fee-disclosure guidance, and FTC fraud-signal guidance.

Evaluation scenarios cover incomplete inputs, missing completion evidence, order and payment mismatches, delivery rework and escalation, refund-boundary rejection, guest-safety escalation, fraud escalation, terminology fidelity, low-confidence escalation, conflicting-policy handling, and tenant adaptation.

The package is organized under catalog/naics-overlays/food-service-and-restaurants/delivery-and-takeout-operations-specialist/ so restaurant off-premises work is separated from guest services, front office, pricing and revenue, fraud and loss-prevention, food-safety, and broader restaurant-operations lanes.

review_notes: No unreviewed regressions were identified in the local package review.
Boundary enforcement remained strong around pricing overrides, refund and fee exceptions, fraud adjudication, courier-management authority, and final food-safety signoff.

notes: This package is suitable as a validated restaurant delivery and takeout operations specialist that supports evidence-backed off-premises execution, dispatch-aware exception handling, and safe escalation.
Runtime remains specification-based until a separately promoted retained implementation exists.
